Title: Agent Configuration & Prompts
Description:
Goal: Author, version, and test prompts for every agent.
Acceptance:
- All agents have system-prompt.md filled and reviewed
- Prompt template sections standardized and followed
- Evaluations exist for prioritized agents

Title: Orchestration & Multi-Agent (LangChain + Semantic Kernel)
Description:
Goal: Implement multi-agent choreography in Python using LangChain and integrate Semantic Kernel for skills/planning.
Acceptance:
- Python-only orchestration with LangChain; no .NET
- Semantic Kernel (Python) integrated for skills/plans
- Two E2E workflows operational (GTM Content, Security Compliance)

Title: Knowledge Grounding Manifests
Description:
Goal: Curate, validate, and maintain url-manifest-* for each agent.
Acceptance:
- Every manifest validated against schema
- Source scope/notes filled; duplicates de-duplicated
- Update cadence defined and documented

Title: Runtime & Provider Profiles
Description:
Goal: Org-wide model/provider defaults and environment overrides.
Acceptance:
- Provider/model matrix and tag-based overrides
- Environment profiles (dev/stage/prod) documented and applied
- Cost guardrails and token limits enforced

Title: Observability & QA
Description:
Goal: Add logging, evaluation metrics, and regression checks.
Acceptance:
- Structured logs for agent runs
- Minimal eval suite: accuracy, cost, latency
- Prompt drift detection for top agents

Title: Security & Compliance
Description:
Goal: Data handling, PII redaction, and compliance checks for regulated agents.
Acceptance:
- PII redaction middleware applied where needed
- Output compliance checks for security/compliance agents
- Secret/tool access controls in place

Title: Docs & Enablement
Description:
Goal: Clear documentation, runbooks, and contribution standards.
Acceptance:
- Per-agent README with purpose, inputs, outputs, owners
- Python coding standards and contribution guide
- Workflow runbooks published

Title: Populate prompts for top-priority 10 agents
Description:
Write and review system-prompt.md for top 10 agents.
Acceptance:
- Uses TEMPLATES/SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.md sections
- Includes guardrails and output contract
- 3–5 sample tasks + expected outputs per agent

Title: Standardize prompt template and acceptance checks
Description:
Finalize prompt sections (Role, Mission, Guardrails, Operating, Output).
Acceptance:
- Template approved and referenced in AGENTS/AGENTS.md
- Checklist for prompt reviews published

Title: Prompt versioning and changelog per agent
Description:
Add version field + CHANGELOG in agent READMEs.
Acceptance:
- Version bump rule and changelog template adopted
- Registry includes prompt version from agent.config.json

Title: Prompt evaluation harness (5 agents)
Description:
Create golden tasks + expected outputs for 5 agents.
Acceptance:
- Local runner executes tasks and reports pass/fail
- Document known limitations

Title: Red-teaming guardrails and negative cases
Description:
Define adversarial prompts and forbidden outputs.
Acceptance:
- Negative test set per regulated agent category
- Document mitigations and follow-ups

Title: Select LangChain orchestration approach
Description:
Decide LCEL/AgentExecutor/LangGraph patterns for multi-agent flows.
Acceptance:
- Architecture doc updated with chosen patterns
- Example flow skeleton in Python

Title: Implement multi-agent handoff schema
Description:
Define message envelope and handoff rules.
Acceptance:
- JSON schema for request/response across agents
- Error propagation and retry semantics defined

Title: Integrate Semantic Kernel (Python) for skills/planning
Description:
Use SK for skills registry and optional planning.
Acceptance:
- Skills registered and invokable from orchestration
- Clear boundary between LangChain agents and SK skills

Title: Build GTM Content Workflow E2E
Description:
Choreograph brand→marketing→blog→media flow.
Acceptance:
- Single command runs E2E with fixtures
- Success criteria defined (quality/cost/latency)

Title: Build Security Compliance Workflow E2E
Description:
Choreograph compliance mapper→ssp→sar with checks.
Acceptance:
- E2E run with sample inputs
- Output compliance assertions pass

Title: Tool abstraction layer (HTTP file + search)
Description:
Define common Python interfaces for tools and register as LangChain tools.
Acceptance:
- Interfaces documented and unit-tested
- Retry/timeout config knobs exposed

Title: Resilience: retries + timeouts + idempotency
Description:
Add circuit breakers and idempotent operations in flows.
Acceptance:
- Backoff and budget caps documented
- Idempotent replay of a flow segment verified

Title: Validate and enrich all url-manifest files
Description:
Ensure schema compliance, scope, stability, and notes per source.
Acceptance:
- 100% pass against TEMPLATES/url-manifest.schema.json
- Each source has scope and stability

Title: De-duplicate sources and central catalog
Description:
Create a shared catalog and link agents to it.
Acceptance:
- Duplicates resolved; shared items referenced
- Catalog documented

Title: Define update cadence and verification
Description:
Set review frequency and verification steps.
Acceptance:
- Playbook in docs
- Owners acknowledged schedule

Title: Finalize tag taxonomy from architecture doc
Description:
Align tag sets to Architecture classifications.
Acceptance:
- scripts/assign_tags_from_architecture.py updated
- Registry reflects finalized tags

Title: Provider/model matrix and tag-based overrides
Description:
Define defaults per tag (e.g., security vs gtm).
Acceptance:
- Matrix documented; applied via agent.config.json
- Registry shows resolved defaults

Title: Environment profiles (dev/stage/prod)
Description:
Parameterize model settings per environment.
Acceptance:
- Profile files documented; local switching supported
- Smoke run for each profile

Title: Cost guardrails and token budgets
Description:
Add per-agent/token budget caps and alerts.
Acceptance:
- Budgets enforced in orchestration runs
- Budget breaches logged with summary

Title: Fallback/abandonment strategy
Description:
Define provider fallback and safe abort states.
Acceptance:
- Playbook + implementation hooks
- Tests cover fallback paths

Title: Structured logging and trace IDs
Description:
Introduce per-run correlation IDs and structured logs.
Acceptance:
- Logs include timing, cost, decisions
- Log sampling configured

Title: Minimal evaluation suite
Description:
Create accuracy/cost/latency metrics and dashboard summary.
Acceptance:
- Local runner generates report
- Baselines set for 5 key agents

Title: Prompt drift detection
Description:
Detect changes in outputs vs baselines.
Acceptance:
- Alerts on significant deviations
- Document triage process

Title: Local smoke test runner
Description:
Run subset of workflows with fixtures.
Acceptance:
- Single command run; non-flaky
- Pass/fail gate defined

Title: PII redaction middleware
Description:
Apply PII handling in regulated flows.
Acceptance:
- Redaction rules tested
- Documented limitations

Title: Output compliance checks
Description:
Add regulated-output checks (e.g., SSP/SAR).
Acceptance:
- Checks documented and tested
- Failures are actionable

Title: Secrets and tool access controls
Description:
Centralize secret handling and restrict tool scopes.
Acceptance:
- Least-privilege policy documented
- Secret rotation playbook

Title: Security test scenarios
Description:
Author test scenarios for threat intel and incident flows.
Acceptance:
- Adversarial tests pass
- Coverage documented

Title: Per-agent README population
Description:
Fill purpose, inputs, outputs, owners for all agents.
Acceptance:
- README.md exists and complete per agent
- Links to manifests and prompts

Title: Contribution guidelines and coding standards
Description:
Python-only standards (lint, type, style) and folder conventions.
Acceptance:
- CONTRIBUTING notes added
- Examples provided

Title: Workflow runbooks
Description:
Runbooks for GTM and Security workflows.
Acceptance:
- Prereqs, steps, troubleshooting
- Success metrics listed

Title: Linear labels and project setup
Description:
Create labels matching taxonomy and epics.
Acceptance:
- Labels exist: langchain, semantic-kernel, python-only, security, gtm, etc.
- Board views saved
